MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANTS MEDICAL DEPOT, INC. DBA DRIVE DEVILBISS HEALTHCARE AND APRIA HEALTHCARE, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' motion to strike punitive damage allegations from the complaint at the pleading stage is denied.

The complaint pleads that when plaintiff sat on the seat of defendants' walker its "left leg broke in half, causing him to crash onto the floor and sustain serious injuries." (Id. at 3:15-19.) After defendants replaced the first walker with one of the same model, plaintiff "went to sit on the seat" and "the rear left leg broke in half in the same place." (Id. at 3:5-9, 21-24.) It is pled that both walkers broke due to a product defect known to defendants. (Id. at 3:27-28.) This suffices to allege punitive damages at the pleading stage. (See Civ. Code 3294.) Corporate knowledge of the defect is inferable from the first walker breaking; in any event, discovery can further develop the facts.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling pursuant to CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not present. Matter not reported.
(302/RBU)
